rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 14, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT ANTHONY AMERICO SIPOS AS AN INDIVIDUAL, AND DBA CUSTOM DOOR COMPANY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Continued on the court's own motion to November 26, 2012 to give Defendants the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Defendants must deliver courtesy copies of its moving papers to Department 302 with a cover letter stating the new hearing date no later than November 14, 2012 at 4 pm, or the motion will be denied. The court has courtesy copies of the opposition and reply papers. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: THEODORE BROWN & PARTNERS, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. DINESH MANIAR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 14, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANT FAR EAST NATIONAL BANK, FRONT STREET BUILDERS, LLC - DOE 16's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: RIMMA BOROKHOV et al VS. FREEDOM WEST HOMES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 14, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 1, PLAINTIFFS RIMMA BOROKHOV, DAVID TSE, MICHAEL TO'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Deny the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: MARK SWENDSEN VS. RETIREMENT BOARD OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To File Petition Under Seal; Memo Of P & A; Dec And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 14, 2012, Line 9, PETITIONER MARK SWENDSEN'S Motion To File Petition Under Seal. Hearing required. No opposition filed. Petitioner shall prepare a proposed order that complies with CRC 2.550(e) and reflects that portions of documents and pages that do not contain materials protected by privilege or statute must be included in the public record as part of a "redacted" filing, as Petitioner appears to have done with the Oct. 9, 2012 "redacted" Petition for Writ of Administrative Mandate. See CRC 2.550(e)(1)(B). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: IRENE GIAMPAOLI VS. TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Vacate Default Ans For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 14, 2012, Line 18, DEFENDANT TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INC.'S Motion To Vacate Default And For Sanctions. Granted. The default entered against defendant on October 15, 2012 is vacated and set aside pursuant to CCP §473(d) because personal service on office receptionist was not proper under CCP § 416.10. Even if personal service were otherwise proper, the Court would vacate and set aside the default judgment under CCP §473(b) on account of mistake or excusable neglect. Sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted by the moving party without need for any appearances. = (302/MJM)
